name: flow/flow-canvas
description: The pan/zoom canvas pane — a single CSS-transformed layer driven by the viewport signal.

meta.name: FlowCanvas
meta.description: The pan/zoom canvas pane — a single CSS-transformed layer driven by the viewport signal.

props.name: viewport
props.type: { x: number; y: number; zoom: number }

props.name: minZoom
props.description: Lower bound for the viewport zoom level.

props.name: maxZoom
props.description: Upper bound for the viewport zoom level.

props.name: panOnDrag
props.description: Drag the empty pane to pan the viewport.

props.name: zoomOnScroll
props.description: Wheel or pinch to zoom the viewport.

props.name: fitView
props.description: Frame the whole graph once on mount.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

whenToUse: Hosting a hand-composed flow from low-level FlowNode/FlowEdge primitives
When you need direct control of the viewport, pan, and zoom
whenNotToUse: For the common case — prefer the declarative <Flow nodes edges /> instead
intent.a11yRationale: role="application" — the pane is an interactive canvas with pointer gestures.
